title: Internals: Implement tagged unions per IEEE 1800-2023 §7.3.2 (#6867 partial)
body:
## Summary

Step 3 of #6867: Implements tagged union support per IEEE 1800-2023 §7.3.2.

Includes partial support for tagged expressions (§11.9) and pattern matching (§12.6) sufficient for common use cases.

This builds on Step 2 (#6881) which added AST node infrastructure for tagged union parsing.

Fork CI passed: https://github.com/10U-Labs-LLC/verilator/actions/runs/21118499524

## Tagged Union Declarations (§7.3.2)

Fully implemented:

- Tagged union type declarations with the tagged qualifier
- Void members
- Packed representation (tag in MSBs, data in LSBs)
- Unpacked representation for dynamic types (real, string, class, chandle)

### Packed vs Unpacked Rationale

IEEE 1800-2023 §7.3.2 specifies that tagged unions with dynamic types cannot be packed. The implementation detects dynamic types (real, string, class, chandle) and generates unpacked struct representations where each member is stored separately. Packed unions use bit operations with tag in MSBs and data in LSBs per the standard.

## Tagged Expressions (§11.9)

Partially implemented:

```systemverilog
typedef union tagged { void Invalid; int Valid; } VInt;
VInt v = tagged Valid (42);
v = tagged Invalid;
```

Limitation: Unpacked unions only work in simple procedural assignments, not in continuous assignments, ternary expressions, or function arguments. Packed unions work in all contexts.

### Rationale

Unpacked unions require member-by-member assignment transformation that currently only handles direct assignment context. Packed unions transform to bit operations that work anywhere.

## Pattern Matching (§12.6)

Partially implemented:

```systemverilog
case (v) matches
  tagged Invalid: $display("invalid");
  tagged Valid .n: $display("valid: %d", n);
endcase

if (v matches tagged Valid .n)
  $display("valid: %d", n);
```

Limitation: Nested tagged patterns like tagged Jmp (tagged JmpU .a) are not yet supported. Simple patterns (.n) and struct patterns ('{field:.var}) work.

### Rationale

Pattern matching transforms to tag extraction and comparison. Pattern variables are extracted from the data portion and bound to local variables. Nested tagged patterns would require recursive transformation which adds complexity for a less common use case.

## Implementation

### V3Tagged Pass

A dedicated transformation pass (V3Tagged.cpp, ~1000 lines) runs after V3Width to lower tagged union constructs into primitive bit operations:

- Tagged expressions: Combine tag bits with member value using shifts and masks
- Case matches: Transform to switch on extracted tag bits, with pattern variables extracted from data portion
- If matches: Transform to tag comparison with pattern variable extraction in the true branch

Separating this into its own pass keeps V3Width focused on type checking while V3Tagged handles semantic lowering. This follows the pattern of other transformation passes like V3Table and V3Tristate.

### V3Width Extensions

Adds ~514 lines for type checking and width calculation:

- AstTaggedExpr: Validates member exists, checks expression type compatibility
- AstPatternTagMem: Validates pattern variable types match member types
- AstCaseMatches/AstIfMatches: Validates expression is tagged union type

Width/type checking runs before transformation so V3Tagged can rely on correct type information.

### Grammar Changes

Extended verilog.y to parse:

- tagged member_identifier [expression] in expression contexts
- matches tagged_union_pattern in case items
- matches tagged_union_pattern in if conditions

The grammar follows IEEE 1800-2023 BNF to ensure correct parsing of legal constructs.

## Test Coverage

PR #6869 added .v tests for tagged union functionality, but these covered success cases only. This PR adds _bad tests to cover error paths:

- t_tagged_case_notunion_bad: Case matches on non-tagged-union type
- t_tagged_if_notunion_bad: If matches on non-tagged-union type
- t_tagged_expr_nocontext_bad: Tagged expression without context type
- t_tagged_expr_wrongtype_bad: Tagged expression with wrong type
- t_tagged_expr_complex_bad: Unpacked union in unsupported context
- t_tagged_matches_notpattern_bad: Matches with non-pattern expression
- t_tagged_member_notfound_bad: Non-existent member in tagged expression
- t_tagged_nonvoid_noexpr_bad: Non-void member without expression
- t_tagged_void_withexpr_bad: Void member with expression

These are required by t_dist_warn_coverage which enforces that every v3error/v3warn message has test coverage.
